calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jul-29-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate Of Alternatively, Application For Alternative Writ Enforcing The California Public Records Act
Off Calendar Jul-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONERS CATALIN VOSS, YUN HONG, KRISTEN BELL, AND NICHOLAS MCKEOWN'S Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate Of Alternatively, Application For Alternative Writ Enforcing The California Public Records Act.

(SEE WORD MINUTES FOR APPEARANCES.)

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

Petitioner Voss's petition for peremptory writ of mandate is granted. The court directs respondent to provide petitioner with records identifying the race and/or ethnicity of each person currently or formerly in the custody of the California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation for whom a parole suitability hearing was held between January 1, 2002 and November 30, 2019.

As more fully explained in the companion case of Brodheim v. CDCR, CGC-20-516978, the cited information is not exempt from disclosure. Gov't Code 6254(k) does not bar the disclosure. The Penal Code does not proscribe the release of the information because it is not "criminal offender record information." 15 CCR 3261.2 is not "federal or state law" that bars disclosure. Gov't Code 6254(c) does not exempt disclosure because respondent fails to show an unwarranted invasion of privacy based on balancing the competing interests at stake.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate Of Alternatively, Application For Alternative Writ Enforcing The California Public Records Act
